local/max-total-depth.flags: Indentation deeper than configured maximum (default 3 levels)
local/max-total-depth.fix: Extract deeply indented blocks into separate functions at module scope
local/max-total-depth.pitfalls: Indent auto-formatting on ternary chains cascades into new depth violations. Nested ternaries beyond 3 branches will always exceed the depth limit
local/max-total-depth.avoid: Nested ternaries beyond 3 branches. Deep if-chains. Inline JSX nesting
local/max-total-depth.philosophy: Bounds structural complexity per generation. Shallow code is locally verifiable — each function readable in one pass without holding nesting context

local/no-nested-function.flags: Parameterized function inside another parameterized function. Two permitted nesting shapes: (1) Nullary PA — inner has zero params and is not parameterized inside parameterized, (2) IIFE thunk — (() => (node) => work)() collapses by the 1 -> T isomorphism
local/no-nested-function.fix: Three solutions: (1) Extract inner function to module scope, pass free variables as explicit args (lambda lifting). (2) Nullary PA: (fn, ...params) => () => fn(...params). (3) IIFE thunk: (() => (node) => work)()
local/no-nested-function.pitfalls: Object literal method shorthands are NOT exempt — { foo(node) { } } is a nested function expression not a method. The makeHandler(fn, ctx)() anti-pattern is a no-op (1 -> T ≅ T) — put the IIFE inside makeHandler. .bind() is never valid
local/no-nested-function.avoid: Closures capturing outer scope. .bind() for partial application — it is not a valid solution. Nested arrow chains beyond the PA boundary
local/no-nested-function.philosophy: Lambda lifting (Johnsson 1985): every closure is mechanically eliminable by lifting free variables into parameters. Flat typed arrows with explicit params — the signature IS the specification

local/restrict-return-count.flags: More than 1 return statement per function, or any non-final return (early return)
local/restrict-return-count.fix: Ternary for 2-3 branches. Nullish coalescing chain (tryA(x) ?? tryB(x) ?? fallback) for T|undefined dispatch. Record dispatch for 4+ branches
local/restrict-return-count.pitfalls: Arrow expression bodies have no ReturnStatement node — invisible to this rule. Guard clauses (if (bad) return) are always flagged even with 1 total return. Do not use switch+let — functional/no-let bans let entirely. Max 3 ternary branches before prettier cascades into max-total-depth. For 4+ use try-dispatch or Record dispatch. Block body wrapping ternary in const+return triggers arrow-body-style — use expression body directly
local/restrict-return-count.avoid: Early returns / guard clauses. Multiple return paths. If-else chains returning from each branch
local/restrict-return-count.philosophy: Single return = total arrow. One input path, one output path, both visible in the type signature. The implementation is determined by the type, not by which branch executes

local/enforce-record-type.flags: Type alias body is an object literal type (TSTypeLiteral)
local/enforce-record-type.fix: Rewrite { key: T } as Record<K, V> or restructure
local/enforce-record-type.pitfalls: Fires on all type literals including call/index/method signatures. For lookup tables needing T|undefined use try-dispatch — Record access never returns undefined
local/enforce-record-type.avoid: Object literal types { key: T }. Use Record<K, V> for keyed structures
local/enforce-record-type.philosophy: There are only two concrete containers: Array (positional) and Record/mapped type (key-indexed). Since constant families are invalid (require-parametric-record), every product is dependent — Record<K, F<K>> or {[K in T]: F<K>}. Object literals are ad-hoc encodings of dependent products

local/max-type-nesting.flags: More than 1 nested type construct (TSTypeLiteral or TSTupleType) in a type alias
local/max-type-nesting.fix: Extract inner type literals and tuples into named type aliases
local/max-type-nesting.pitfalls: Counts occurrences, not depth. Two sibling type literals at the same level both increment the counter
local/max-type-nesting.avoid: Inline anonymous type structures within type aliases
local/max-type-nesting.philosophy: A.(B.(C.(...))) = A.X where X = B.(C.(...)). Nested type composition always factors through a named intermediate — the extraction is the factorization made explicit

local/no-duplicate-type-structure.flags: Two or more type aliases with identical canonical structural representation
local/no-duplicate-type-structure.fix: Remove duplicate, reuse the existing type alias. If semantically distinct, differentiate the structure
local/no-duplicate-type-structure.pitfalls: Uses module-level Map that persists across files in a lint run. clearFile handles re-linting. Canonical string comparison is nominal — renamed but structurally identical types are still caught. TSNamedTupleMember labels collapse canonical forms — [node: A] and [node: B] both canonicalize to [TSNamedTupleMember]. Remove labels to expose actual types, or share one tuple for identical param signatures. Cross-file collisions with generic tuple wrappers — TSingleArg<T> collides with same-shape tuples in other files. Keep per-file named tuples with distinct unlabeled inner types. Phantom generic params to differentiate trigger valid-generics — share identical tuples or eliminate redundant functions instead
local/no-duplicate-type-structure.avoid: Introducing new type aliases without checking for existing structurally identical types
local/no-duplicate-type-structure.related: enforce-record-type, require-extracted-types, max-type-nesting, consistent-type-definitions, require-rest-params-tuple, valid-generics
local/no-duplicate-type-structure.philosophy: Two types with identical structure are the same type. Catches redundancy at lint time per-issue before duplicates enter the codebase and diverge across issues

local/no-single-field-type.flags: Type alias with exactly one member in a type literal body (except call signatures)
local/no-single-field-type.fix: Inline the single field type directly or combine with other fields
local/no-single-field-type.pitfalls: Call signature types ({ (x: A): B }) are exempt — they are canonical function type form, not wrappers
local/no-single-field-type.avoid: Single-property wrapper types. type TFoo = { value: string } — use string directly
local/no-single-field-type.philosophy: Every type must earn its existence structurally. A single-field wrapper adds indirection without information

local/require-extracted-function-type.flags: Inline type annotations that are not keywords or named references (TSTypeReference)
local/require-extracted-function-type.fix: Extract to a named type alias: type TFoo = string | number; const x: TFoo = ...
local/require-extracted-function-type.pitfalls: High volume — a function with 3 params + return + 2 locals could need 6 type aliases. Naming requires semantic thought, not mechanical substitution. Inline tuple types (TSTupleType) in annotation position are flagged — extract to named alias first. Lambda-lifting cascades into require-rest-params-tuple — new function signature needs its own tuple type. Do minimal fix only unless also targeting no-nested-function
local/require-extracted-function-type.avoid: Inline unions, intersections, function types, tuple types, object literals in annotation position
local/require-extracted-function-type.philosophy: Every value has a named type defined before the value exists. The type vocabulary IS the specification — readable without any implementation

local/require-parametric-record.flags: Record or mapped type without dependent product structure (value type does not reference key type parameter)
local/require-parametric-record.fix: Add type parameter: type TFoo<T extends X> = Record<T, TBar<T>>. For mapped types: {[K in T]: F<K>}. Or replace with tuple if no parametric relationship exists
local/require-parametric-record.pitfalls: Record check is regex-based on source text. Mapped type check uses AST. Complex formatting may not match regex. Value must be wrapped in a named type for Records
local/require-parametric-record.avoid: Non-parametric Records like Record<string, number>. Degenerate mapped types like {[K in T]: string} where value ignores the index
local/require-parametric-record.philosophy: Products indexed by a finite domain must be dependent — the value must reference the index. Both Record<T, F<T>> and {[K in T]: F<K>} are dependent products. Degenerate forms collapse to constant families

local/valid-generics.flags: Degenerate generic (body is just the type parameter: type TId<T> = T) or homogeneous generic (all params passed straight through: type TFoo<A,B> = TBar<A,B>)
local/valid-generics.fix: Degenerate: remove type parameter, use inner type directly. Homogeneous: remove generic params (make simple alias) or add transformation logic
local/valid-generics.pitfalls: Detection uses string comparison of parameter names — renaming alone defeats the check. Intentionally nominal, not deep structural equivalence
local/valid-generics.avoid: Identity functors. Pure-passthrough generics that add a name without adding a transformation
local/valid-generics.philosophy: Every generic must earn its existence by transforming its parameters — adding constraints, composing, partially applying. Trivial generics pollute the type graph with vacuous edges

local/require-rest-params-tuple.flags: Type-level function signature with positional parameters instead of rest-params tuple
local/require-rest-params-tuple.fix: Wrap parameters in a named tuple type and spread: type TArgs = [a: A, b: B]; type TFn = (...args: TArgs) => R
local/require-rest-params-tuple.pitfalls: Zero-param signatures () => R are valid and not flagged. Currently type-level only — runtime signatures commented out. Extracting structurally identical tuples for different functions triggers no-duplicate-type-structure — share one tuple when param signatures match. Inline tuples are TSTupleType not TSTypeReference — rule rejects them, must extract to named type alias. Type predicates (node is T) incompatible — no named param in rest-params form. Delete predicate, inline discriminant check instead. .bind() typing unreliable with rest-params tuples — TS may infer any[]. Use IIFE thunk instead. Single-element tuples are safe from no-single-field-type — that rule only checks TSTypeLiteral not TSTupleType
local/require-rest-params-tuple.avoid: Inline positional parameters in type-level function signatures. Named individual params like (a: A, b: B)
local/require-rest-params-tuple.related: require-extracted-types, transport-graph, valid-generics, no-duplicate-type-structure, no-single-field-type, enforce-record-type
local/require-rest-params-tuple.philosophy: Every function is an arrow A -> B between named types. Rest-params tuple makes the input a single named product, enabling mechanical comparison and transport graph edges

local/finite-domain-return-widening.flags: Function from finite domain returns bare 'string' — prefer a literal union return type
local/finite-domain-return-widening.fix: Replace bare string return with a literal union that maps each input variant to a specific output. The function becomes a lookup table
local/finite-domain-return-widening.pitfalls: Only checks TSFunctionType inside TSTypeAliasDeclaration. Does not check implementation return values, only type-level annotations
local/finite-domain-return-widening.avoid: Returning bare string from functions whose input is a finite domain (literal or literal union)
local/finite-domain-return-widening.philosophy: Finite domains should propagate. If the input space is enumerable, the output space should be too. Makes the function decidable and exhaustively testable

local/type-distance.flags: Two type aliases with structural similarity >= 0.75 (Jaccard similarity on keys and values)
local/type-distance.fix: Merge the two types into one if they represent the same concept, or add/remove fields to make the structural difference explicit
local/type-distance.pitfalls: Uses module-level mutable array for cross-type comparison. Exact duplicates (canonical match) are excluded — use no-duplicate-type-structure for those
local/type-distance.avoid: Near-duplicate type aliases with high Jaccard similarity that should be unified or made explicitly different
local/type-distance.philosophy: The type namespace is a set — no two names should describe the same shape. Near-duplicates indicate concept drift that should be resolved

local/cardinality-isomorphic-families.flags: Two generic types with the same constraint that have identical cardinality profiles (union members, record fields, tuple elements)
local/cardinality-isomorphic-families.fix: Unify the two generic types into a single parameterized type if they agree pointwise over their shared domain
local/cardinality-isomorphic-families.pitfalls: Only compares first type parameter constraint. Uses module-level mutable array
local/cardinality-isomorphic-families.avoid: Generic type families over the same finite domain with identical cardinality profiles that are secretly isomorphic
local/cardinality-isomorphic-families.philosophy: Let F, G : S -> FinSet be functors over a finite discrete category S. If |F(a)| = |G(a)| for all a in S, then F ~ G (naturality is vacuous over discrete S). The natural isomorphism witnesses a factorization: exists H : S x I -> FinSet with H(-,i0) ~ F and H(-,i1) ~ G — the two families are fibers of a single family parameterized by the missing index I

local/transport-graph.flags: Discriminated type with no edges in the transport graph — not used as domain or codomain of any function signature
local/transport-graph.fix: Define a function type that uses the isolated type as domain or codomain: type TFn = (x: TIsolated) => TTarget
local/transport-graph.pitfalls: Only checks within a single file. Exports buildTransportGraph and classifyEdge for fiber-coherence. string keyword return (TSStringKeyword) won't create edges — rule requires TSTypeReference. Return a named type not a keyword. Rest-params form (RestElement) won't create edges — fails param check. Must use inline params. CONFLICTS with require-rest-params-tuple
local/transport-graph.avoid: Discriminated types (literal unions or constrained generics) that exist without any function signature connecting them
local/transport-graph.philosophy: Types are nodes, functions are edges. The type graph must be connected — every type participates in the computation graph. Isolated nodes are dead types

local/fiber-coherence.flags: Two types connected by an isomorphic edge in the transport graph that have different cardinalities
local/fiber-coherence.fix: Either adjust the types to have matching cardinalities (same number of variants/fields/elements) or reclassify the edge as a section
local/fiber-coherence.pitfalls: Depends on transport-graph exports. Duplicates several helpers (TAliasEntry, isLiteralType, findConnected, etc.) — should extract to shared module
local/fiber-coherence.avoid: Isomorphic edges between types with mismatched cardinalities — a bijection between sets of different size is a contradiction
local/fiber-coherence.philosophy: Structural promises must be backed by cardinality evidence. An isomorphism between a 3-variant union and a 5-variant union is a proof obligation that cannot be satisfied

@typescript-eslint/ban-ts-comment.fix: Remove the directive. Fix the underlying type error instead
@typescript-eslint/ban-ts-comment.philosophy: Types are provable contracts. Overriding the checker voids the contract

@typescript-eslint/ban-tslint-comment.philosophy: Mechanical cleanup of legacy suppression directives

@typescript-eslint/consistent-generic-co.flags: Generic type args on constructor call instead of type annotation
@typescript-eslint/consistent-generic-co.fix: Move generics to the variable declaration: const x: Array<T> = new Array()
@typescript-eslint/consistent-generic-co.avoid: const x = new Array<T>() — put generics on the left side
@typescript-eslint/consistent-generic-co.philosophy: Type annotation is the contract; right-hand side is implementation

@typescript-eslint/prefer-function-type.flags: Type literal with only a single call signature
@typescript-eslint/prefer-function-type.avoid: Unnecessary structural wrapping of pure function types
@typescript-eslint/prefer-function-type.philosophy: Reduce indirection for pure function types

@typescript-eslint/array-type.fix: Use Array<T> syntax. Tuples are still allowed
@typescript-eslint/array-type.pitfalls: Rule appears twice in config — deduplicate
@typescript-eslint/array-type.philosophy: One canonical form for containers. Array<T> is consistent with Record<K,V>

@typescript-eslint/consistent-type-asser.flags: Any type assertion (as X or <X> syntax)
@typescript-eslint/consistent-type-asser.fix: Remove assertion. Restructure code so TypeScript infers the correct type. Use switch dispatch or try-dispatch pattern
@typescript-eslint/consistent-type-asser.pitfalls: Combined with no-unsafe-type-assertion, there is truly no way to assert
@typescript-eslint/consistent-type-asser.avoid: All type assertions. Use type narrowing, switch dispatch, or try-dispatch instead
@typescript-eslint/consistent-type-asser.philosophy: Assertions are lies to the type checker. Every type must be provably correct through inference

@typescript-eslint/no-unsafe-type-assert.flags: Type assertions that narrow to a more specific type
@typescript-eslint/no-unsafe-type-assert.fix: Use type narrowing via discriminated unions or switch
@typescript-eslint/no-unsafe-type-assert.pitfalls: Closes both directions with consistent-type-assertions: never
@typescript-eslint/no-unsafe-type-assert.philosophy: Every value must be the type it claims through inference alone

@typescript-eslint/consistent-type-defin.fix: Convert to type alias: interface IFoo becomes type TFoo
@typescript-eslint/consistent-type-defin.philosophy: One canonical form. Types are explicit, composable via intersection, fixed at definition site

@typescript-eslint/consistent-type-impor.pitfalls: Update imports when value import becomes type-only after refactor
@typescript-eslint/consistent-type-impor.avoid: Importing types without the type keyword
@typescript-eslint/consistent-type-impor.philosophy: Separates type-level and value-level dependency graphs

@typescript-eslint/consistent-type-expor.flags: Type exports without export type keyword
@typescript-eslint/consistent-type-expor.avoid: Exporting types without the type keyword
@typescript-eslint/consistent-type-expor.philosophy: Separates type-level and value-level export graphs

@typescript-eslint/explicit-function-ret.flags: Function without explicit return type annotation
@typescript-eslint/explicit-function-ret.fix: Add : TReturnType. Create the type alias first if needed
@typescript-eslint/explicit-function-ret.pitfalls: Combined with require-extracted-types, return type must be a keyword or named reference
@typescript-eslint/explicit-function-ret.avoid: Relying on type inference for return types
@typescript-eslint/explicit-function-ret.philosophy: Every function advertises its output type. The stub is a binding contract

@typescript-eslint/typedef.fix: Add explicit type: const x: T = ...
@typescript-eslint/typedef.pitfalls: Combined with require-extracted-types, complex types need named aliases
@typescript-eslint/typedef.avoid: Implicit type inference on variable declarations
@typescript-eslint/typedef.philosophy: No inference = no ambiguity. The type is the contract, stated explicitly

@typescript-eslint/no-deprecated.fix: Replace with the current API equivalent
@typescript-eslint/no-deprecated.philosophy: Keeps codebase current. Prevents agents from generating code against outdated APIs

@typescript-eslint/no-duplicate-type-con.flags: Redundant members in unions (A | A) or intersections (A & A)
@typescript-eslint/no-duplicate-type-con.avoid: Duplicate members in union or intersection types
@typescript-eslint/no-duplicate-type-con.philosophy: A union with a duplicate member is not minimal. Canonicality at the constituent level

@typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any.flags: Usage of any type (except rest args)
@typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any.fix: Replace with a specific type or unknown
@typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any.avoid: The any type in all positions except rest arguments
@typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any.philosophy: any is a hole in the type system. Every binding must have a real type contract

@typescript-eslint/method-signature-styl.flags: Method signature syntax foo(x: A): B in type literals
@typescript-eslint/method-signature-styl.fix: Use property style: foo: (x: A) => B
@typescript-eslint/method-signature-styl.avoid: Method signature syntax in type literals
@typescript-eslint/method-signature-styl.philosophy: Functions are values. Property form is consistent with const arrow pattern

@typescript-eslint/no-unnecessary-condit.flags: Conditions that are always truthy or always falsy
@typescript-eslint/no-unnecessary-condit.fix: Remove condition. Use try-dispatch for T | undefined lookups
@typescript-eslint/no-unnecessary-condit.pitfalls: Record<K,V>[k] returns V not V | undefined. Use try-dispatch for the undefined case
@typescript-eslint/no-unnecessary-condit.avoid: Unnecessary ?? or ? on types that cannot be nullish
@typescript-eslint/no-unnecessary-condit.philosophy: Precise types mean fewer branches. Tighten the type to eliminate dead code

@typescript-eslint/no-confusing-void-exp.fix: Use statement form. Arrow shorthand is exempt
@typescript-eslint/no-confusing-void-exp.avoid: Void return values in expression position
@typescript-eslint/no-confusing-void-exp.philosophy: Void values should not appear where a value is expected

eslint-comments/no-use.fix: Fix the underlying issue structurally. No suppression allowed
eslint-comments/no-use.philosophy: No escape hatches. If lints pass, the code conforms to the algebra, period

functional/no-let.fix: Use const. For dispatch: try/nullish-coalescing chain (tryA(x) ?? tryB(x) ?? fallback)
functional/no-let.pitfalls: No exceptions. switch+let is banned — use nullish coalescing chains or Record dispatch instead
functional/no-let.philosophy: Values do not change. Immutability makes reasoning local. Every binding is a fact stated once

arrow-body-style.flags: Block body when expression body suffices
arrow-body-style.fix: Remove braces and return keyword: => expr
arrow-body-style.pitfalls: Concise arrows have no ReturnStatement node — invisible to restrict-return-count
arrow-body-style.avoid: Block body ({ return expr; }) when => expr suffices
arrow-body-style.philosophy: Expression-oriented code. A concise arrow is a pure mapping from input to output

func-style.fix: Convert to const arrow: const f: T = (...) => ...
func-style.avoid: function declarations. Use const arrow expressions
func-style.philosophy: Functions are values. const bindings with types, not hoisted declarations

prefer-arrow-callback.philosophy: Complement to func-style: expression. All functions are arrows

indent.fix: Restructure code to reduce indentation. Extract into smaller functions at module scope
indent.pitfalls: Indent auto-formatting on ternary chains cascades into max-total-depth errors
indent.avoid: Deep nesting. Ternary chains that push indentation beyond the depth limit

max-len.fix: Break into multiple lines. Use string concatenation for long strings. Extract sub-expressions
max-len.pitfalls: Only URLs are exempt. Strings and template literals are NOT exempt
max-len.avoid: Long lines. Template literals that cannot be broken across lines
max-len.philosophy: Forces decomposition. A long line is a complex expression that has not been named

max-lines-per-function.flags: Functions exceeding 40 lines (including blanks and comments)
max-lines-per-function.fix: Extract helper functions at module scope
max-lines-per-function.pitfalls: Counts blank lines and comments. Combined with no-nested-function, helpers go to module scope
max-lines-per-function.philosophy: Hard ceiling on generation size. Every function fits on a screen and is reviewable in seconds

complexity.fix: Extract branches into functions. Use try-dispatch pattern
complexity.pitfalls: Each if, else if, case, &&, ||, ??, and ternary ? adds 1
complexity.avoid: Deep branching logic. If-chains with many conditions
complexity.philosophy: Bounds decision space per function. Nearly linear logic — cheap per-function verification

no-warning-comments.fix: Remove comment. Restructure code to work with prettier
no-warning-comments.philosophy: No escape hatches for formatting. Fix code structure, not suppress the formatter

no-restricted-syntax.flags: Type predicates (is keyword in return type)
no-restricted-syntax.fix: Use discriminated unions or explicit type narrowing
no-restricted-syntax.philosophy: Type predicates are refinement assertions — narrowing that depends on implementation correctness. Use discriminated unions for structural, decidable narrowing

id-length.fix: Use descriptive names. Exceptions: id, i, j, k, x, y, z, _, fs, db, ui, el, e
id-length.avoid: Short identifiers outside the exceptions list
id-length.philosophy: Descriptive names carry meaning across issues. Self-documenting functions reduce context needed downstream
